message:
test(fsqlite-pager): self-allocated EOF page test gains NoopWalBackend + full WalBackend trait surface

Follow-up to the previous commit. The integration test asserted the
post-commit state against the pager's in-memory VFS only, which left
the cross-process conflict-prediction path partly untested — the real
contract wants to see a `WalBackend` attached to the pager so the
`predicted_conflict_pages_for_wal_commit_with_inner` call runs through
its full branching.

Add a `NoopWalBackend` stub that implements every `WalBackend` method
(`append_frame`, `append_frames`, `prepare_append_frames`, `read_page`,
`committed_txn_count`, `sync`, `frame_count`, `checkpoint`,
`conflicting_pages_since_snapshot`) as a no-op, and attach it to the
pager before the test runs the Concurrent transaction. The stubbed
methods return `Ok(())` / `Ok(None)` / `Ok(vec![])` so the pager
happy-path is exercised without spinning up a real WAL file.

Imports the extra trait types (`CheckpointPageWriter`,
`CheckpointResult`, `CheckpointMode`, `WalBackend`) needed for the
stub. The rest of the test body (begin Concurrent, allocate past
EOF, assert no conflict surface, commit) is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
